item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, that's a nice bit of fridge logic there. I'm a sucker for anomalies that just happen to reveal something else deeper going on, completely unintentionally from the original purpose/intent.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

With three black-boxed SCPs at the end of this skip, there is ample room here for a crosslink. Havuing had a chance to speak with Drewbear on the subject, SCP-1182 came up. This object, which lets anyone who hold it turn invisible, and makes them think they are an octopus, certainly may hold interest to the invisible octopus like entities that appear in SCP-1828. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

Addendum: Two years after initial acquisition, the rate of appearance of SCP-1828-A resembling A. marginatus has increased to 12.4% of all such occurrences, and appear within the containment chambers of SCP-███, SCP-████, and the current host of SCP-████ approximately 63.2% of the time. Chief Researcher A. Bjornsen has requested that SCP-1828 be transferred to a remote single-SCP Site in order to minimize potential exposure of Foundation assets to whatever entities SCP-1828-A represents. While this request is under review, the Head of Security has requested additional resources to more extensively monitor the aforementioned SCP Items.

To this:

Addendum: Two years after initial acquisition, the rate of appearance of SCP-1828-A resembling A. marginatus has increased to 12.4% of all such occurrences, and appear within the containment chambers of SCP-███, SCP-1182, and the current host of SCP-████ approximately 63.2% of the time. Chief Researcher A. Bjornsen has requested that SCP-1828 be transferred to a remote single-SCP Site in order to minimize potential exposure of Foundation assets to whatever entities SCP-1828-A represents. While this request is under review, the Head of Security has requested additional resources to more extensively monitor the aforementioned SCP Items.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Took a second to understand this correctly. I suggest it be brushed up a little bit for clarity. Otherwise it's a fun object with an interesting backbone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What does it mean :0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would love to see a cross test exploration with SCP-178, to "see" if those figures gain representation inside SCP-1828.

Edit: I was bugged and finally looked up what the glass figures reminded me of, the Victorian age Blaschka blown glass deep sea animal models. Short version, deep sea critters were being discovered, but were too fragile and rare to be studied by everyone who wanted to. Thus this awesome Glassblower was commissioned to recreate them in (comparatively) sturdy glass.

http://designmuseum.org/design/leopold-rudolf-blaschka

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They take a variety of forms ranging in size from 0.3 cm to 5.2 cm, and primarily consist of hexapodal felinoids, airborne vermians or ophidians, and representatives from multiple species within the Dasypodidae family averaging 4.1 cm in height.

If you wanted to say that the glass figures looked like worms you should have used the term "vermiform" not "vermian" as vermian is not really used.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I stand corrected. My apologies Mr. Boots, I did not mean to steal your "first".

But I still think that the article should address the alternative hypothesis. In-universe, it makes the most sense for the powers that be to act as if the threat is real, but officially acknowledge the possibility that it is not. This not only gives all staff the clearest possible picture of the situation, it gives the administration a way to save face if glitching is ever proven to be the case.

It costs them nothing, is good scientific methodology, and could make the difference between appearing appropriately cautious and appearing needlessly paranoid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, FuzzyBoots made the same point on page 1. And yes, that is an equally valid interpretation. But 'tis better to err on the side of caution, after all…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why is it taken for granted that the -As represent real entities?
Unless and until there is some independent corroborating evidence of these phantom octopi/armadillos, it is equally likely that they are just a glitch in the modeling mechanism.
I could see the Foundation taking a security-centered "assume the worst until proven otherwise" approach, but I also think that they would at least note the alternative hypothesis in that case.

I also find it interesting that, judging by the comments, I am the first reader to think this way. Makes me wonder what I'm missing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It confused me, too.
My first interpretation of "17:37" was "37 minutes past 1700 hours".
I know that's not how military time is written, but it's where my head went without conscious consideration on my part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is correct.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hate this article, delete it

This statement was written 100% with the consent of this insignificant human being and in no way influenced by the Great Many-Legged Ones that are NOT present or influencing any human being

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for explaining what this is and what it does in here, Drewbear. I was quite confused when I read the article. Now I can give you your well-earned upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I read it right, no, it doesn't. The 1828 'replica' is a solid piece of wood, not a box.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Question: does it repeat fractally? As in: is the replica of SCP-1828 found in SCP-1828 an instance of 1828?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I assumed it would stay in its current state until it is placed in a proper location for updating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't specify that, no. Any foreign objects inside the dollhouse when it updates are moved outside of it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait. Question. I may be missing the answer to this in the article, but I cannot for the life of me find it. What happens to things when you put something inside it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Weird and confusing. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Charming, and just weird enough for an upvote. I have to wonder what happens if you store it outside.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 17:37, meaning 17 minutes and 37 seconds. Confusing, I take?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to put 184 in it, just to see what happens.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Every hour, at 17:37 past the hour,"

^This needs fixing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It leaves me bemusedly pondering out stories about the invisible entities (not just the octopi), and I like SCPs that lead me into that sort of extended speculation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, I kind of like the idea that they have nothing to do directly with the dollhouse, but instead are the result of a slight glitch in the process. When I first read it, I was vaguely reminded of some sci-fi story involving a Lovecraft-level ghostly monster that turned out to be a greatly magnified view of a micro-organism and completely harmless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Das cute. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They definitely appear to be interacting with us, but we don't know that they're manipulating us. Also note that, at first, they barely showed up, but now they make up about 1 in 8 of all of the glass models. So something is attracting them to the Foundation Site that currently houses SCP-1828, and that attraction apparently only began after SCP-1828 was placed there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really should wait at least thirty minutes after waking up before reading articles. Okay, now I understand the size changing thing as well as the designations. However, if the octopodes aren't even manipulating/influencing things… well, that just makes me care even less.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting. Short, but interesting. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well there's only two designations:

  • SCP-1828 is the shapeshifting box
  • SCP-1828-A are the glass models, some of which are octopus-shaped

The regular miniatures don't get designations because they are/appear to be completely non-anomalous.

As for the shapechanging nature of the box: Right now it's in your home, reflecting the floorplan of that level of the building (including other apartments, if you're in an apartment block), at 1/12 the size of the floorplan. Before it next updates, you put it in a garden shed in the backyard, and during the next update the whole thing shrinks to show just the shed, at 1/12 scale. Then you take it to your high school and during the next update it expands to reflect the floor plan of the school, at 1/12 scale. The update is instantaneous, so it's not like you can see it shrink or grow, it's just becomes a different size during the update.

As for the octopodeal things, who said that they're manipulating you? ;-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes! Exactly like that!

:-| IGNORE ME!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do like the purely logical perspective of "Finding out I am the plaything of a non-euclidean alien does not change anything because I have ALWAYS been the plaything of a non-euclidean alien". Like if you found out that our universe is simply a simulation of a universe being run in a "higher order" universe. It doesn't invalidate your reality, because there was no reason to have validated it to being with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like this?

SCP-1828 is uniformly represented by a 3" x 3" x 3" (7.62 cm x 7.62 cm x 7.62 cm) solid cube composed of the same wood that forms the exterior walls of SCP-1828.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is moderately confusing at most and slightly cliche at least. It felt like you referred to several things as "SCP-1828" whereas I kept (and continue to) envision the box and the models it makes as two separate things, I'm not clear on what exactly happens during expansion, and the idea that there are space octopodes manipulating my movements doesn't really interest me because, hey, they've probably been doing that for a while and nothing extraordinarily bad has happened so they must be doing something right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel the article should mention if/how SCP-1828 is itself is manifested within SCP-1828, and whether or not there is recursion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While the second part of the skip is old as dirt (SCP-1042 and From Beyond immediately spring to mind), the way the box works is neat and well thought-out, so I'll upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvote for yet another "This is the SCP but the real SCP is the SCP within this SCP (although we won't bother actually doing any research on that or explaining anything about it)".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a lot. The only weird thing I noticed was that you used Imperial units when describing the size of the representative cube for the scip (3" x 3" x 3"), but I can overlook that because you used SI immediately after that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neat. It's as if the box takes a snap shot of where it is, makes a model of it an includes all living things there, including entities which may be invisible. Makes me wonder if the octopi 1828-A are controlling the researchers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Time to play dress-up with your dolls! Isn't it amazing how lifelike they are?
